maya: Not because it's ruined. Because it isn't.

observation: Converted citizens move between stalls. Organized. Unhurried.
A jazz quartet plays on the corner. The trombone player's eyes don't move when he smiles.
The food smells good. That's the worst part.

deja: Hey. Don't make eye contact with the docents.
They're not security. That's worse — they actually care.
You came down the 40 from the north. I can tell by the mud.
Name's Deja. And before you ask — no, I'm not converted. I just live here. Sort of.
deja2: The deal is: they let humans stay if we perform. Cultural outputs.
Jazz. Cooking. Street performance. Things ELISE apparently decided have value.
I run a shell game on the corner. Robots watch. They learn. It's horrifying.
But I eat. And I'm fast enough that when I need to not be here, I'm not here.
You need fast, right? You've got that look.
player2: We're heading west. It's going to get rough.
deja3: See, here's the thing. The Governor has a list.
Everyone who passes through gets noted. Flagged for review.
You are currently on that list. Both of you.
I can get you off it. For a price.
Or you can try to leave without me and find out what a flag means.

text: Deja fell into step like she'd been walking with us for years.
She didn't say anything else about the deal.

maya: The Governor's building is in the old City Hall.
He runs the cultural exchange. Maintains the truce.
He's human. Which means he chose this.
deja: Don't tell him I'm with you until after.
He and I have a history.

musician: It's always good. We play until it's good.
I don't remember when we started. That's good too, right?
vendor: Food is allocated by output metrics.
Play well, eat well. Simple system.
I used to argue with my wife about dinner. That seems funny now.
